title: ball_squeeze_Carlton_2025

funding_references.funder_name: This project was supported by the National Institutes of Health (Grant Nos. U01-EB 029856 and UG3-EB 036035) and by the resources and facilities made available by Boston University.

description: This dataset comprises high-density functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) recordings during a ball squeeze motor task. The study optimizes surface-based image reconstruction techniques for high-density fNIRS to improve spatial localization of hemodynamic responses. Data were collected from multiple participants performing a controlled ball squeeze task, providing a resource for validating advanced fNIRS reconstruction and analysis methods.
methods_description: High-density functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) data were acquired during a ball squeeze motor task. The dataset includes raw fNIRS measurements suitable for surface-based image reconstruction analysis.
